bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just beginning (t=0.0) and I am making the first bid, I will start by proposing the outcome that maximizes my utility. My preferred value across all issues is 'valueC', so I will request this to establish a strong opening position.

The negotiation is in its very early stages, and the opponent has proposed an offer at the literal opposite end of my preference spectrum (suggesting they favor valueA across the board). To signal a willingness to negotiate while preserving near-maximum utility, I will make a minor concession on one of my least important issues (issueA) by moving it to valueB, keeping the most critical issues at valueC.

The negotiation is very early at t=0.04, and the opponent has repeated their opening offer of minimal utility (all valueA). To signal willingness to negotiate without significantly sacrificing overall utility, I will make a small concession on my lowest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB), while holding firm on the higher-weighted issues (issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF).

The opponent is repeatedly anchoring on their opening offer of all 'valueA'. Since the time fraction is still extremely early (t=0.06), I need to gently explore their preferences without sacrificing much utility. I will fully concede on one of my least important issues (issueA) to see if giving them their preferred value on this issue prompts any reciprocal concession, while maintaining my most important issues at their maximum utility values.

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.08), but the opponent has remained completely unyielding, demanding 'valueA' across all issues in every turn. I will continue to make small, measured concessions on my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) to encourage reciprocity, while holding firm on my highest-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD) where I receive my maximum utility.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their extreme opening offer, consistently bidding 'valueA' for all issues despite my concessions. At t=0.10, which is still very early in the negotiation, I will fully concede on my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB to valueA) to demonstrate flexibility and see if this prompts any reciprocation. I will firmly hold my highest-weight issues (issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF) at valueC to protect my core utility, maintaining an overall target utility of 0.84.

I am holding firm on my most important issues (issueD, issueE, issueF) at my highest utility value (valueC) because we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.12). Since the opponent has not deviated from proposing their maximum utility position (all valueA) on every turn, I need to slow my concessions. I will propose valueB for my lower-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to signal flexibility while keeping my overall utility high to invite actual reciprocity.

The opponent is employing a purely hardline strategy, repeating the exact same offer requesting 'valueA' for all issues despite my repeated concessions. Since the time fraction is still early (t = 0.14), I will avoid dropping my utility too precipitously. I will concede fully to 'valueA' on my least important issues (issueA and issueB), partially concede on issueC, and hold firm on my most important ones (issueD, issueE, and issueF) to protect my core utility while demonstrating flexibility.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, exclusively bidding valueA for every issue since the beginning. Because time is still early (t=0.16) and I have made several unilateral concessions without reciprocation, I need to slow down my concessions. I will offer a bid that grants them valueA on my least preferred issues (A and B) while slightly compromising on issueD to explore tradeoffs.

The opponent is holding firm with a rigid offer giving me minimum utility (all `valueA`) consistently. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I will slowly concede on moderate priority issues while keeping my top priorities (especially `issueF`) at maximum utility. In this bid, I am offering `valueA` on my lowest-weighted issues, and making a moderate concession on `issueE` to `valueB` to signal flexibility, maintaining an overall utility around 0.86.

The opponent has repeatedly proposed the identical offer (all `valueA`) across every single turn, demonstrating extreme rigidity early in the negotiation. Realizing they are uncompromising, I will bounce my utility demand slightly back up and explore different issue tradeoffs to test if the opponent has variable preferences across the issues. I will hold firm on my most highly weighted issue (issueF) and offer concessions on lower-weight issues like issueA and partial concessions on issueD and issueB.

The opponent is holding extremely firm by repeating the exact same offer (valueA for all issues) which gives me a very low utility. Since the time fraction is only 0.22, there is no need to make significant concessions yet. I will maintain high utility on my most heavily weighted issues (issueF, issueD, issueE) while exploring tradeoffs on the lower-weight ones to see if the opponent can be drawn out of their rigid position.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on their initial offer of all 'valueA's without making any concessions. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will maintain a high-utility offer for myself (>0.9) while exploring different combinations of minor concessions on issues A, B, and E to see if it prompts any reciprocal movement. I will hold firm on my highest weighted issue (issueF, weight 0.3).

The opponent continues to hold firm on their opening offer of `valueA` for all issues without making any concessions. Because we are still in the early phase of negotiation (t=0.26), I will hold firm on my highest-valued issues (`issueF` and `issueE`) and explore trade-offs by offering another intermediate combination. By fully yielding on my lowest-priority issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) and offering intermediate values on `issueC` and `issueD`, I aim to coax a meaningful counter-offer while maintaining a high personal utility (~0.78).

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their original position (valueA for all issues) without offering any concessions. Since the time fraction is still low (t=0.28), I must be careful not to concede too rapidly while still trying to explore different combinations of values to find a mutually acceptable package. I am now providing their preferred value on my lowest importance issues (issues A and B), and partially conceding on issues D and E, while maintaining my highest utility on my most important issue (issue F).

The opponent represents a hardline attitude, repeating the exact same bid since the beginning of the negotiation which yields my minimal possible utility. Since the time fraction is still very early (t=0.30), I will continue maintaining a high utility threshold for myself. I am conceding on the lower-weight issues (issueA and issueB) while retaining my ideal value on my highest-weight issues (issueF and issueD) and partial utility on issueC and issueE in order to see if the opponent will finally demonstrate some flexibility.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on `valueA` for all issues. To find a potential compromise while still maintaining high utility early on (t=0.32), I will concede outright on `issueD` to `valueA` in addition to my lowest-weight issues (`issueA` and `issueB`). This gives them their preferred value on three issues, preserving my utility on my most important ones (`issueF`, `issueC`, `issueE`) and keeping my total expected score around 0.78.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on `valueA` for all issues without any concessions or messages. Given that `time_fraction` is 0.34, I will continue to systematically test lower-priority issues by offering `valueA` to see if it yields any flexibility from the opponent, while maintaining `valueC` on my highest-priority issue (`issueF`) and other high-value issues. I will now concede `issueE` to `valueA` while holding `issueC` and `issueD` at `valueC`.

The opponent is holding extremely firm and repeating the exact same bid since the beginning. Since time is at t=0.36, I have room to patiently explore tradeoffs without dropping my target utility too drastically. I am fully conceding on my least important issues (issueA, issueB) as well as issueC to the opponent's preferred value, while holding firm on my highest-weight issues (issueD, issueE, issueF) to test if they will accept a balanced compromise.

The opponent has repeatedly offered the same bid (valueA for all issues) without making any concessions up to t=0.38. I will continue to explore the search space by conceding on some of my medium-weight issues (issueC and issueD) while holding firm on my most important issue (issueF) and another medium-weight issue (issueE) to invite a counteroffer. This maintains my utility around 0.70 while systematically testing what the opponent might accept.

The opponent remains completely inflexible, proposing the exact same offer of `valueA` for every issue since `t=0.02`. I will continue to hold firm on my most important issue, `issueF`, at `valueC`, while proposing a slightly more conceded mid-game trade. I will offer `valueA` on my lowest-weighted issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) and test an even mix of `valueB` on the middle-weight issues (`issueC`, `issueD`, `issueE`) to see if the opponent has a preference amongst them.

The opponent remains completely rigid, repeatedly offering all `valueA`, which is their best bid and terrible for me. Since we are at `t=0.42`, I should continue testing substitutions in the mid-weight issues (C, D, E) to see if they'll bite, while conceding my low-weight issues A and B fully, and retaining my highest-weight issue F at `valueC`. This offer maintains a solid utility for me (~0.70) while heavily conceding E to probe their preferences.

The opponent is heavily stonewalling, offering only `valueA` for every single issue without moving. Since we are approaching the midway point (t=0.44), I will continue accommodating their preference for `valueA` on select issues where my weight is moderate or low, in hopes of breaking their deadlock. I will maintain `valueC` on issueF, which holds the greatest significance to my utility, while dropping issueC entirely to their favored `valueA`.

The opponent remains completely rigid, repeating the exact same offer (all valueA) entirely in their favor without any concessions. As we are approaching the midway point of the negotiation (t=0.46), I will continue to explore different middle-ground options by offering 'valueA' on my low-weight issues (A and B) and 'valueB' on some moderately weighted issues like C and D, while holding firm on my highest-priority issue (F) and E. This secures a strong utility for me (~0.78) while matching the opponent's values on two issues.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on `valueA` across all issues, showing no willingness to concede so far. Since we are approaching the halfway mark (t=0.48), I will continue to explore combinations that concede on my lower-weight issues while holding firm on my most important issue (issueF). I will offer `valueA` on issues A, B, and C to appease them, but keep D, E, and F at higher utility levels to maintain an overall utility above 0.7.

The opponent has stubbornly offered 'valueA' for every issue with zero concessions up to t=0.50. Given that time is now at the midpoint, I need to start signaling flexibility without giving up my core value. I will concede fully to their preferred 'valueA' on my lowest weight issues (issueA, issueB) while offering intermediate 'valueB' on most mid-weight issues, attempting to find a workable middle ground that still preserves over 0.7 utility for myself by holding firm on issueF.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, demanding their ideal outcome (`valueA` on all issues) across the entire history, despite my continuous concessions on lower priority issues. Since time is at 0.52, I will continue to hold firm on my most important issue (issueF at valueC) while exploring a new combination of partial concessions on my mid-weight issues (issueD to valueA, issueE to valueB) to see if the opponent is willing to make a trade-off.

The opponent is holding completely firm at their opening offer, stubbornly requesting `valueA` for every issue. Since we have passed the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.54) and the opponent hasn't budged, I need to start exploring new combinations that incorporate `valueA` for some issues while protecting my most valuable issues, especially issueF and issueE/D. I will concede issueA, issueB, and issueD to `valueA` entirely to signal a willingness to cooperate, while holding issueF and issueE at top values.

The time fraction is 0.56, meaning we are just past the midpoint. The opponent has rigidly bid their top value ('valueA') on every single issue for the entire negotiation so far and has not conceded. Thus, I will try lowering my target utility to ~0.62 to see if giving them 'valueA' on several issues (A, B, C) and 'valueB' on others (D, E) will induce them to accept, while keeping my most critical issue (F) firmly at my top preference ('valueC').

The opponent has rigidly held to a single offer of 'valueA' for all issues throughout the negotiation. Since we are around the mid-game (t=0.58), I need to continue exploring trade-offs to see if conceding fully on low-to-medium weight issues prompts a change in their position. I will offer 'valueA' on issues A, B, and E, while maintaining 'valueC' on my most important issue F and high-weight issue D.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, consistently proposing 'valueA' for everything without making any concessions. As time advances past the midpoint, I need to start yielding 'valueA' on my lower-priority and mid-priority issues (like issues A, B, and D) to find a compromise, while firmly holding 'valueC' on my most important issue (issueF) to preserve my minimum acceptable utility. This bid targets a utility of approximately 0.62, bringing us closer to their demands to break the deadlock.

The opponent has finally shown a slight concession on issueD by moving to valueB, while holding firm on valueA for everything else. Since we are entering the later stages of the negotiation at t=0.62, I will lower my demand on my highest-weighted issue (issueF) from valueC to valueB to signal flexibility and encourage reciprocal concessions. To maintain a strong utility (around 0.74), I will ask for valueC on issueC and issueE while matching their offer on issueD.

The opponent typically insists rigidly on `valueA` for all issues, but briefly conceded `issueD` to `valueB` at t=0.62. To encourage further movement and build on this small concession, I will offer `valueB` for `issueD` and `issueC`, and `valueA` on my low-weight issues (`issueA`, `issueB`). I am holding firm at `valueC` on my highest-weight issues (`issueE`, `issueF`) to maintain a strong utility around 0.78 as we pass the midway point.

The opponent has consistently insisted on `valueA` for almost all issues, but has now twice signaled a concession on `issueD` to `valueB` (at t=0.62 and t=0.66). I will actively meet them on `issueD` and concede to `valueA` on some issues where my weight is lower, like `issueB` and `issueE`. However, to protect my overall utility, I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues, particularly `issueC` and `issueF`.

The opponent is showing extreme stubbornness, insisting mostly on 'valueA' for all issues, but recently made a minor concession on issueD by proposing 'valueB' at t=0.62 and t=0.66. To signal flexibility while protecting my most critical issues (issueF and issueE), I will continue accommodating their interest in 'valueA' on lesser issues and incorporate their concession 'issueD: valueB'. I am adjusting issueA and issueC to explore new combinations that maintain my utility around 0.74.

The time fraction is 0.7, signaling the late stage of the negotiation. The opponent has repeatedly offered all `valueA` but has recently started conceding on `issueD` by proposing `valueB` multiple times. I will reinforce this concession by setting `issueD` to `valueB` and keeping the low-weight issues `issueA` and `issueB` at their preferred `valueA`. To maintain my utility above 0.75, I will offer a compromise on `issueE` while holding firm on the highest-weight `issueF` and `issueC`.

The opponent has recently started to concede on `issueD` to `valueB`, indicating a potential area of compromise. At t=0.72, we are in the late game, so I must strategically adjust my offers to accommodate their preference while maintaining decent utility for issues important to me (`issueF`, `issueE`, `issueC`). I will match their concession on `issueD` and propose `valueB` on `issueC` and `issueE`, while holding firm on my most important issue, `issueF`, ensuring overall utility stays above 0.7.

The time fraction is currently 0.74, and the deadline is approaching. The opponent has been highly uncooperative, stubbornly offering valueA for all issues, but has recently begun proposing valueB for issueD. To encourage agreement while still preserving my utility, I will accept their proposal of valueB for issueD and concede to valueA on my least important issues (A, B, and C). I will hold firm on valueC for my most heavily weighted issues (E and F) to ensure my overall utility remains above 0.70.

The opponent has shown strong rigidity, consistently demanding `valueA` for all issues, but has recently shown flexibility by conceding to `valueB` on `issueD`. To find a mutually acceptable agreement as we approach the final stages of the negotiation, I will accept their concession on `issueD` and offer them their preferred `valueA` on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueE`. In exchange, I will maintain `valueC` on my most important issue (`issueF`) and `issueC`, keeping my utility at a reasonable ~0.70.

The opponent has rigidly requested `valueA` on most issues, but recently showed a willingness to yield to `valueB` on `issueD` and `issueF`. At t=0.78, I will grant them `valueA` on `issueA` and `issueB` (my lowest priority issues) and accept their tentative concession to `valueB` on `issueF` and `issueD`. In exchange, I am targeting higher utility for myself on `issueC` and `issueE`.

The current time is 0.80, so we have a little bit of time left to find a compromise. The opponent has recently begun making concessions (issueD = valueB, issueF = valueB, and just now issueC = valueB). I will accept their latest move on issueC, keep the issues they really want at valueA (issues A and B), and hold firm on my highest priority issues (E and F) to maximize my utility while still moving closer to their preferences.

The opponent just made a very significant concession by offering `valueC` on `issueF`, which is my most important issue (weight 0.3). They have previously signaled flexibility on `issueC` and `issueD` as well. To capitalize on this and build towards a consensus, I will lock in `issueF: valueC` while making partial concessions on `issueD` and `issueE` to `valueB`, aiming for a compromise that preserves a strong utility of ~0.78.

With the deadline approaching (t=0.84), I see the opponent has made a significant concession on my highest weight issue, `issueF`, offering `valueC`. In previous rounds (t=0.62-0.80), the opponent also signaled willingness to move to `valueB` on `issueC` and `issueD`. I am proposing a compromise that solidifies their concession on `issueF` while mirroring their previous willingness to accept `valueB` on mid-weight issues, aiming to construct a mutually acceptable agreement.

The opponent has recently shown willingness to concede on issueF (to valueC at t=0.82 and t=0.84) and on issueC and issueD (to valueB at t=0.86). Given the time fraction of 0.86, I will combine these past localized concessions into a single bid. This yields a reasonable utility of 0.624 for me while strongly overlapping with what they have already demonstrated they are willing to accept.

The time fraction is 0.88, meaning we are approaching the deadline. The opponent has recently shown a willingness to concede on issueF (offering valueC at t=0.82 and t=0.84), issueC and issueD (offering valueB at t=0.86), and issueE (offering valueB at t=0.88). To maximize my utility while finding a plausible agreement, I will adopt a combination of these recent concessions by dropping issueC to valueA (which they want) while retaining issueE and issueD at valueB and issueF at valueC, maintaining a solid utility above 0.6 for myself.

The time is at 0.90, which requires making significant concessions to secure an agreement. The opponent recently showed willingness to offer `valueC` on `issueD` and `issueF`, as well as `valueB` on `issueC` and `issueE` at varying points. I will combine their accepted concessions, prioritizing my most valuable issues `issueF` and `issueD`, while dropping `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueE` down to `valueA`. This aims for an outcome that meets both sides' established flexibility fields.

The time is 0.92, meaning we are close to the deadline. The opponent's recent offers show a willingness to concede on issueD (valueC) and previously on issueF (valueC). To reach an agreement quickly while preserving my highest utilities, I will concede fully on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueE (all to valueA), while requesting my preferred values for issueD and issueF (valueC), which they have both accepted in parts of their previous bids.
